name: Pulpí - Terreros Trail
name_original: San Juan de Terreros – sendero a Pulpí hasta Mirador del Apartadero
description: This difficult 13.8-mile (22.1 km) hiking trail offers diverse coastal and inland scenery, including beaches and the Apartadero Viewpoint.
region_intro: Hiking around Pulpí offers a diverse landscape, combining coastal scenery with mountainous terrain and unique geological formations. The region features fine yellow sand beaches, sea-eroded caves, and coastal paths. Inland, the Sierra del Aguilón provides more challenging routes with panoramic views. The area also encompasses scrubby hills and agricultural zones, offering varied environments for exploration. Best hiking trails around Pulpí The most popular hiking route is Tour of Calarreona beaches, a 4.3 miles (6.9 km) trail that takes 1 hour 47 minutes to complete. This easy route features easily accessible paths along the coast. Another top favourite among local hikers is Pulpí - Terreros Trail, a difficult 13.8 miles (22.1 km) path. This route offers diverse coastal and inland scenery, including beaches and viewpoints. Local hikers also love the San Juan de los Terreros Beach – Terreros Beach loop from Los Baldíos, a 4.6 miles (7.3 km) trail leading through coastal paths with sea views, often completed in about 1 hour 53 minutes. Hiking around Pulpí is defined by its coastal paths, mountainous terrain, and unique geological formations. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to challenging mountain trails. The routes in Pulpí are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 120 reviews. More than 600 hikers have used komoot to explore Pulpí's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Pulpí?
A: Pulpí offers a diverse selection of over 30 hiking trails, catering to various preferences and skill levels. These routes explore the region's unique blend of coastal scenery, mountainous terrain, and geological formations.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Pulpí?
A: Pulpí's Mediterranean climate makes hiking enjoyable for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for exploring both coastal paths and inland trails. Winter can also be a good time for hiking, especially along the coast, while summer might be very hot, making early morning or late afternoon hikes preferable.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Pulpí?
A: Yes, Pulpí has plenty of easy options, with over 15 routes classified as easy. A great choice for families or beginners is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e898118419/tour-of-calarreona-beaches" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour of Calarreona beaches&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 6.9 km trail with accessible paths along the coast, perfect for a relaxed stroll.

Q: Are there challenging hikes in Pulpí for experienced hikers?
A: Absolutely. For experienced hikers seeking a challenge, the region offers routes with significant elevation changes, particularly in the Sierra del Aguilón. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983224919/pulpi-terreros-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pulpí - Terreros Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 22.1 km path that provides diverse coastal and inland scenery, including beaches and panoramic viewpoints, requiring good fitness.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Pulpí?
A: Pulpí's hiking terrain is incredibly varied. You can expect fine yellow sand beaches and coastal paths with stunning sea views, as well as more rugged mountainous terrain in areas like the Sierra del Aguilón. Trails also traverse scrubby hills and agricultural zones, offering a rich mix of environments for exploration.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Pulpí?
A: Yes, several routes in Pulpí are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31534274" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Juan de los Terreros Beach – Terreros Beach loop from Los Baldíos&lt;/a&gt;, a 7.3 km trail that takes you along coastal paths with beautiful sea views.

Q: What natural attractions or landmarks can I see while hiking in Pulpí?
A: Pulpí is rich in natural beauty and unique landmarks. Along the trails, you can discover picturesque spots like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4085357" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Cocedores Beach&lt;/a&gt;, known for its unique sea-eroded caves, or enjoy expansive vistas from &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5312101" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cala Cristal Views&lt;/a&gt;. The region is also home to the world-renowned Pulpí Geode, a spectacular geological wonder, which can be integrated into your outdoor itinerary.

Q: Are there coastal walks with beautiful sea views in Pulpí?
A: Pulpí is renowned for its stunning coastal trails. Many routes offer breathtaking sea views and access to beautiful beaches such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4201747" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Higuerica Beach&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5267593" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Juan de los Terreros Beach&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31815725" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Juan de los Terreros Beach – La Higuerica Beach loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 13.2 km route that highlights the best of the coastline.

Q: Can I hike with my dog in Pulpí?
A: Many trails in Pulpí are suitable for hiking with dogs, especially those in natural areas and along the coast. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in protected areas or near wildlife, and to check local regulations for specific beaches or natural parks you plan to visit.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Pulpí?
A: The hiking experience in Pulpí is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 120 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, from the beautiful coastal paths and beaches to the challenging mountain trails of the Sierra del Aguilón, as well as the unique geological features found in the area.

Q: Are there any hikes that feature unique geological formations?
A: Yes, Pulpí is famous for its geological wonders. Beyond the world-renowned Pulpí Geode, which is an accessible underground attraction, many coastal trails feature unique sea-eroded caves, particularly around &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4085357" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Cocedores Beach&lt;/a&gt;. These natural formations add a fascinating element to your outdoor exploration.

Q: Is parking generally available at trailheads in Pulpí?
A: Parking availability varies depending on the specific trailhead. In popular coastal areas and near major attractions, you'll often find designated parking spaces. For more remote trails, roadside parking might be an option. It's advisable to check specific route details or local information for parking recommendations before heading out.
tips: Intermediate point on the PR-A 232 between Pulpí and Terreros.
From here you have a good view of the two main beaches of San Juan de Terreros: Playa Mar Tranquila and Playa Mar Rabiosa.

San Juan de los Terreros is the last or first coastal town in Andalusia, being right on the border with Murcia. Off its coast are two small islets of volcanic nature that form the Isla de Terreros-Isla Negra Natural Monument.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Pulpí - Terreros Trail?
faqs.answer: This route is considered a difficult hike, spanning approximately 22.1 km with an elevation gain of around 324 meters. It requires a very good level of fitness due to its length and varied terrain.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers a diverse experience, transitioning from the stunning coastline around San Juan de los Terreros with its beautiful beaches and cliffs, to inland areas characterized by agricultural landscapes and sections of the Sierra del Aguilón mountain range. You'll encounter a mix of coastal paths and more rugged, natural terrain.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll discover several notable features. The trail passes by the Apartadero Viewpoint, offering panoramic vistas. You can also enjoy a View of Playa Mar Tranquila and Playa Mar Rabiosa, and the route concludes near the beautiful San Juan de los Terreros Beach. Inland, the famous Giant Gypsum Geode of Pulpí is a significant geological marvel, though not directly on the trail, it's a key regional attraction.

faqs.question: Is the Pulpí - Terreros Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself doesn't have specific restrictions against dogs, it's a long and challenging route. Always ensure your dog is fit enough for the distance and terrain, and remember to keep them on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near agricultural land. Be sure to carry enough water for both yourself and your pet.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The Pulpí - Terreros area experiences hot summers. For a more comfortable hiking experience, the best times to visit are during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) when temperatures are milder and the natural scenery is vibrant.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Pulpí - Terreros Trail. It is generally accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The Komoot route for the Pulpí - Terreros Trail typically starts near Pulpí and heads towards San Juan de los Terreros. Parking availability can vary, but you can usually find public parking in the towns at either end of the route. It's advisable to check local parking options in Pulpí or San Juan de los Terreros before you go.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I see along the way?
faqs.answer: The diverse landscape supports a variety of wildlife. Inland, you might spot raptors, partridges, foxes, and owls. Along the coast and near the volcanic islets, aquatic birds and seagulls are common. The marine environment also boasts rich ecosystems, including Posidonia meadows.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other hiking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route largely follows the official Pulpí - Terreros trail for about 90% of its length. It also briefly intersects with sections of the Paseo Marítimo and Boulevard trails, particularly as it approaches the coastal areas.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restrooms near the trail?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself is mostly natural, you'll find amenities in the towns at either end. San Juan de los Terreros offers various cafes, restaurants, and shops where you can find refreshments and use facilities. There's also a recreational area with a museum at 'La Cortijá' along the path between Pulpí and San Juan de los Terreros.

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Given its 'difficult' rating, 22.1 km length, and significant duration (around 6 hours), this specific route is generally not recommended for families with young children or those new to hiking. Shorter, easier coastal walks in the San Juan de los Terreros area might be more suitable for a family outing.

waypoints.name: View of Playa Mar Tranquila and Playa Mar Rabiosa
San Juan de los Terreros Beach
